rationale: P0 coverage is 4% (required: 100%). 44 critical requirements uncovered. Verdict identical to AM trace because test-review's CRIT-1 + CRIT-2 fixes addressed test-suite quality (suite green-rate 89% → 100%; Wave 1a adopted test-ID prefixes), not catalog automation (no new scenarios authored). The catalog-automation gap is unchanged: only Wave 1a (4 of 47 P0 scenarios) is delivered. Distinct from a correctness FAIL (which would imply existing tests are red on intended behavior — they are not, all 113 are green). Next-wave automation is now unblocked: CRIT-1 fix removed the TeacherDashboard test-suite blocker that would have made any new test additions appear red.

deltas_vs_prior_run.verdict_change_reason: Test-review fixes addressed infrastructure quality (CRIT-1 localStorage shim, CRIT-2 partial test-ID adoption); no new catalog scenarios were automated, so coverage thresholds are unchanged.

next_workflow_recommendation: bmad-testarch-automate (Wave 1b) — NOT bmad-testarch-atdd; ClassPoints is brownfield with no formal story scaffolding, per handoff §'Recommended BMAD → TEA Workflow Sequence'.
